techniques.name: quorum intersection exploitation
techniques.description: reduce the number of quorums or quorum checks by exploiting the fact that any two quorums of size > n/2 share at least one member
preconditions: protocol uses majority quorums
two phases both require quorum agreement
techniques.generalized_form: if phase A collects a quorum Q_A and phase B collects a quorum Q_B, and both are majority quorums, then Q_A and Q_B share at least one member. any property established by Q_A is visible to Q_B through the intersection, potentially eliminating an explicit transfer step.
techniques.applicability_notes: low: spec uses single-primary-per-scope with one backup, not quorum voting. no majority quorum structure present in CE/OB/OMS.

techniques.name: fast path optimization
techniques.description: skip a protocol phase when a supermajority (e.g. 3/4) agrees, falling back to the normal path otherwise
preconditions: protocol has at least 2 phases
a supermajority threshold exists where the extra phase is provably unnecessary
trust_requirements: supermajority availability for the fast path
techniques.generalized_form: if a phase exists only to resolve conflicts, and a larger quorum guarantees no conflict can exist, skip that phase when the larger quorum responds. maintain the slow path as fallback.
techniques.applicability_notes: low: spec is single-primary replication, not multi-phase voting. no supermajority structure to exploit.

techniques.name: speculative execution
techniques.description: execute operations speculatively before full commit, rolling back if the speculation proves wrong
preconditions: operations are deterministic
rollback is possible and cheap
speculation is correct in the common case
techniques.generalized_form: if a replica can predict the outcome of a pending decision with high probability, execute the operation before the decision is final. maintain a rollback log. if the prediction is wrong (rare), roll back and re-execute.
techniques.applicability_notes: medium: OB buffers fills in ob_pending_log before CE confirmation (pending_seq namespace). this is already a form of speculative buffering. question is whether OB can speculatively apply pending fills before MFR arrives from CE.

techniques.name: request batching
techniques.description: batch multiple client requests into a single consensus instance to amortize per-instance overhead
preconditions: requests are independent or can be ordered deterministically within a batch
batch size bounded to prevent latency spikes
techniques.generalized_form: if the protocol runs one consensus instance per request, and instance overhead is O(n) messages, batch k requests into one instance. per-request cost drops from O(n) to O(n/k). tradeoff: latency increases by batch fill time.
techniques.applicability_notes: high: spec effects are individually sequenced (seq field). batching multiple effects per broadcast message is directly applicable without changing protocol semantics.

techniques.name: consensus pipelining
techniques.description: overlap consecutive consensus instances so that instance N+1 begins before instance N completes
preconditions: instances are independent or have well-defined ordering
the protocol can handle out-of-order completion
techniques.generalized_form: if consensus instances are sequential and each takes R rounds, pipeline up to P instances so that P instances complete in R + P - 1 rounds instead of P * R rounds.
techniques.applicability_notes: medium: CE produces effects sequentially (ce_seq increments monotonically). pipelining CE effect production before backup acknowledgment could increase throughput. spec currently does not require backup ack before next effect.

techniques.name: view change log merging

source.ref: Ongaro & Ousterhout, Raft, 2014; Liskov & Cowling, VR Revisited, 2012

techniques.description: during view change, the new leader merges logs from a quorum to determine the authoritative log suffix, instead of requesting the full log from every replica
preconditions: logs are prefix-consistent (common prefix + divergent suffix)
quorum intersection guarantees the committed prefix is present in every quorum
techniques.generalized_form: if replicas have prefix-consistent logs and only the suffix diverges, the new leader needs only the suffix from a quorum (not the full log from all replicas). committed entries are in every quorum by intersection, so only uncommitted entries need reconciliation.

known_failures.reason: ce_checkpoint_seq persists across view changes, MAX anchor exceeds new session range causing sequence gap

techniques.applicability_notes: high: CEViewChange already implements this via anchor + MaxContiguousFrom(anchor+1, archiver_seqs). the archiver acts as the log source. optimization question: can the merge be made cheaper or the anchor computed more precisely?

techniques.name: uncertified DAG commit via pattern matching
techniques.description: skip certificate collection entirely and derive commit decisions from DAG reference patterns alone
preconditions: DAG topology is sufficiently dense
commit rule is locally verifiable from DAG structure alone
techniques.generalized_form: if a protocol collects certificates before committing, and the same availability guarantee can be inferred from DAG structure, skip certificate collection.
techniques.applicability_notes: low: spec is not a DAG protocol. CE/OB/OMS use linear logs with single leader.

techniques.name: authority-endorsed monotonic leader transitions
techniques.description: require external authority endorsement (signature + strictly-increasing timestamp) for leader transitions, preventing phantom leaders without requiring consensus among downstream services
preconditions: external authority can reliably produce monotonic timestamps
downstream nodes can verify authority signatures
all leadership transitions must carry strictly fresher timestamp than cached
techniques.generalized_form: if leader L broadcasts leadership claim, downstream D caches (leader_id, authority_timestamp). a new claim from L' is accepted only if authority_timestamp' > authority_timestamp and authority_signature is valid.
techniques.applicability_notes: directly instantiated: ce_authority_ts, ob_authority_ts, oms_authority_ts are all authority timestamps. ob_cached_ce_ts and oms_cached_ce_ts implement the downstream caching pattern. already exploited; analysis should check whether all downstream rejection conditions are correctly implemented.

techniques.name: deterministic leader assignment via directory iteration
techniques.description: assign leaders deterministically by iterating over a registered operator map, eliminating election consensus overhead entirely
preconditions: operator set is registered and known to all participants
deterministic tie-breaking rule exists
single leader per resource scope
techniques.generalized_form: if leader election requires a consensus round and the operator set is stable and known, replace election with deterministic iteration: leader(scope) = operators[hash(scope) % len(operators)].
techniques.applicability_notes: medium: CEViewChange increments ce_primary (session counter) deterministically without election. this pattern is already applied but could be made more explicit or extended to OB/OMS scope assignment.

techniques.name: N-way independent path attestation for censorship attribution
techniques.description: route messages through N independent attested relays to make censorship attributable
preconditions: N independent relays available
relays produce send/receive attestation certificates
N is large enough relative to expected collusion threshold
trust_requirements: at least one honest relay in the N paths
techniques.generalized_form: with N independent paths all carrying TEE-attested send certificates, if all N fail: P(N independent operators all collude) is negligible for large N.
techniques.applicability_notes: low: spec does not model relay networks or censorship attribution. archiver redundancy (f+1) is a simpler availability guarantee.

techniques.name: non-equivocation elimination

source.ref: Chun et al., Attested Append-Only Memory, 2007; Levin et al., TrInc, 2009

techniques.description: remove protocol mechanisms that exist solely to detect or prevent equivocation, since TEE attestation already prevents it
preconditions: all relevant nodes run in TEE
TEE provides non-equivocation guarantee (monotonic counters or attested append-only memory)
techniques.generalized_form: for each mechanism M in the protocol, check if M exists to detect equivocation. if TEE already prevents equivocation, M is redundant. remove M, verify properties still hold.

known_failures.reason: Out-of-order backup application breaks ArchiverGCByAcks which depends on MaxOfSet(backup_applied) being a contiguous prefix for safe GC. Gap seqs lost when broadcast+backup fail after GC.

techniques.applicability_notes: high: trust model explicitly notes TEE non-equivocation (D101 applicable). spec uses session numbers to detect stale messages from demoted leaders. question: are there additional mechanisms in the spec that exist only to handle equivocation that TEE already prevents?

techniques.name: signature elimination under attestation

source.ref: TEE consensus literature, e.g. MinBFT, CheapBFT

techniques.description: replace per-message signatures with TEE attestation, since the TEE guarantees message authenticity
preconditions: sender runs in TEE
TEE attestation proves message origin
receiver can verify TEE attestation
techniques.generalized_form: if a protocol uses signatures to prove message authenticity, and the sender runs in a TEE that attests all outgoing messages, the signature is redundant. remove signatures, replace with attestation checks where needed.
techniques.applicability_notes: high: trust model explicitly notes D102 applicable. spec models session numbers as implicit sender identity. question: are there explicit signature fields or signature-checking steps in the spec that could be eliminated?

techniques.name: Byzantine-to-crash fault model reduction

source.ref: Veronese et al., MinBFT, 2013; Kapitza et al., CheapBFT, 2012

techniques.description: when all nodes run in TEE, reduce the fault model from Byzantine to crash-fault, since TEE prevents arbitrary behavior
preconditions: all protocol participants run in TEE
TEE prevents arbitrary code execution
TEE provides non-equivocation
techniques.generalized_form: if a protocol assumes Byzantine faults and requires 3f+1 replicas, but all replicas run in TEE, reduce from 3f+1 to 2f+1 replicas and simplify all quorum thresholds accordingly.
techniques.applicability_notes: already applied: trust model explicitly states 'Byzantine-to-crash reduction already applied (D103 already exploited)'. spec uses crash-only fault model throughout. no further reduction available.

techniques.name: trusted monotonic counter exploitation
techniques.description: use TEE-provided monotonic counters to enforce ordering and prevent replay without protocol-level mechanisms
preconditions: TEE provides a monotonic counter that cannot be rolled back
protocol has replay or ordering concerns
techniques.generalized_form: if a protocol includes mechanisms to detect replayed messages or enforce ordering, and the TEE provides a monotonic counter, replace protocol-level ordering mechanisms with counter values.

applications.notes: ob_authority_ts eliminated. Inductive equality ob_authority_ts = ob_primary trivially proven. Zero property surface (ob_authority_ts absent from all invariants except P1 type check). Clean state variable reduction: removes one per-market Nat from state vector.

known_failures.reason: ce_authority_ts appears in P6/P17 as semantic constraint. VC message ts field would carry session value, creating semantic inconsistency. CE-only elimination without broader refactor (OB/OMS + VC message ts rename + INV6/INV17 rewrite) leaves spec worse.

techniques.applicability_notes: high: authority_ts fields (ce_authority_ts, ob_authority_ts, oms_authority_ts) serve as monotonic counters for leader transitions. cached timestamps (ob_cached_ce_ts, oms_cached_ce_ts, oms_cached_ob_ts) implement the staleness rejection. question: could TEE-native counters replace the protocol-level authority_ts tracking?

techniques.name: liveness attestation for adaptive quorums
techniques.description: use TEE liveness attestation to dynamically adjust quorum sizes based on how many replicas are known to be alive
preconditions: TEE can attest replica liveness
quorum intersection property holds at the minimum adaptive quorum size
techniques.generalized_form: if a protocol uses fixed quorum size Q, but TEE liveness attestation reveals k replicas are currently live, the effective quorum can be Q' = ceil((k+1)/2).
techniques.applicability_notes: low: spec has no quorum structure. CE/OB/OMS each have one primary + one backup (not a quorum). liveness attestation not applicable in this topology.

techniques.name: VRF-free leader rotation via TEE confidentiality

source.ref: ~/projects/heytdep.github.io/drafts/33)--pub)--Diving into Leader Rotation Mechanisms for TEE-Backed Clusters.md:59-93

techniques.description: replace VRF or SSLE-based leader election with deterministic selection inside TEE, where TEE confidentiality prevents external prediction of the next leader
preconditions: small permissioned quorum (typical n < 20)
TEE integrity and confidentiality already trusted
leader unpredictability is desired
techniques.generalized_form: if leader election uses cryptographic randomness to prevent prediction, and all nodes run in TEE, replace VRF with deterministic XOR-based tie-breaking on attested peer set.
techniques.applicability_notes: medium: view changes in spec increment session counter deterministically (ce_primary + 1). no VRF in use. applicable if spec were extended to multi-candidate leader selection. currently limited scope.

techniques.name: asymmetric role specialization (TEE executors + untrusted collectors)

source.ref: ~/projects/heytdep.github.io/drafts/38)--pub)--TEEs for Trustless Complex Data Workflows For Blockchain Apps.md:92-96

techniques.description: split system roles into untrusted collectors and TEE executors, reducing TEE infrastructure cost
preconditions: safety-critical operations are separable from I/O-heavy operations
collectors can be verified by executors
collector failure is a liveness issue, not a safety issue
techniques.generalized_form: split into: (1) collectors on untrusted hardware (networking, data gathering), (2) executors in TEE (state transitions, validation, signing). collector compromise affects liveness, not safety.
techniques.applicability_notes: directly instantiated: archivers (non-TEE) = collectors; CE/OB/OMS (TEE) = executors. trust model explicitly states 'Archivers: non-TEE. Availability-only faults'. already fully exploited. analysis should verify the collector/executor boundary holds under all failure modes.

techniques.name: shared TEE key for light client verification amortization

source.ref: ~/projects/heytdep.github.io/drafts/31)--pub)--Understanding Helios and Exploring Light Clients' Affinity to TEEs.md:339-349

techniques.description: use a shared signing key across all TEE replicas so light clients verify one bootstrap attestation quote instead of per-replica attestation
preconditions: all replicas run identical attested code
shared key distribution mechanism exists
light client trust model accepts 'any valid TEE replica'
trust_requirements: TEE code integrity across all replicas sharing the key
techniques.generalized_form: with a shared key derived from attested code hash, clients verify 1 quote and accept any message signed with the shared key. verification cost drops from O(n) to O(1).
techniques.applicability_notes: medium: spec does not model light clients explicitly, but downstream consumers (OB consuming CE effects, OMS consuming CE and OB effects) verify session/timestamp authenticity. shared key could amortize per-effect verification for downstream.

techniques.name: TEE-signed effect attribution for untrusted storage
techniques.description: sign each state mutation with TEE key over (sequence, session, hash(payload)), enabling untrusted archivers to store and serve effects without being able to forge, reorder, or tamper with them
preconditions: leader runs in TEE and signs all outputs
signature commits to sequence number
payloads are encrypted
techniques.generalized_form: if effects are signed as sig(seq, session, H(payload)) by TEE, forgery is impossible, reordering is detectable, content is hidden. archivers need only be available, not trusted.
techniques.applicability_notes: high: spec effects carry (type, seq, session) fields. archivers are non-TEE. session field ties effect to specific primary (prevents cross-session forgery). question: does the spec commit seq into an authenticated field? recovery via CEArchiverSeqsForSession relies on session fidelity from untrusted archivers.

techniques.name: surgical rollback via per-action nonces
techniques.description: replace blanket state rollback during failover with precision rollback indexed by per-action nonces, only reverting the unrecovered tail of the action log
preconditions: actions carry monotonic nonces or idempotency tokens
actions are independently replayable given correct ordering
recovery process can determine the maximal contiguous prefix of recovered actions
trust_requirements: recovered actions are authentic (signed or attested)
techniques.generalized_form: if a leader produces actions with monotonic nonces, and recovery finds the maximal contiguous prefix (a_1, ..., a_k), then only actions after a_k need rollback.

applications.notes: Added ob_checkpoint_seq as fallback anchor in OBViewChange. MinOfTwo cap prevents cross-session staleness. Structural claim substantiated.

techniques.applicability_notes: directly instantiated: CEViewChange computes anchor = max(backup_applied or checkpoint_seq), then recovered = MaxContiguousFrom(anchor+1, archiver_seqs). ce_seq restarts at recovered+1. this is surgical rollback. analysis should verify the anchor selection is always safe.

techniques.name: separation of data availability from replication
techniques.description: decouple data availability from replication, allowing each to be optimized independently
preconditions: DA layer can store data independently of replica set
replicas can bootstrap from DA layer if all backups are lost
data is self-authenticating
trust_requirements: at least one DA node is available within recovery window
techniques.generalized_form: separate: backups exist for fast failover (hot standby), DA nodes exist for durability (cold archive). losing all backups loses speed, not data.
techniques.applicability_notes: directly instantiated: ce_backup_applied (hot replication) and ce_archiver_store (DA) are separate. CEViewChange uses archivers when backup is insufficient. trust model: 'f+1 redundancy: one honest archiver sufficient for recovery'.

techniques.name: separate sequence namespace for unvalidated actions
techniques.description: use a dedicated monotonic counter for buffered unvalidated actions to avoid sequence ambiguity during recovery
preconditions: service produces actions during periods when upstream validator is unavailable
recovery process must distinguish validated from unvalidated actions
normal sequence namespace is shared between validated and unvalidated entries
techniques.generalized_form: introduce C' (separate counter) for the unvalidated stream. on recovery, the new upstream processes C'-sequenced actions as a clean, unambiguous batch.
techniques.applicability_notes: directly instantiated: ob_pending_seq and ob_pending_log are separate from ob_seq and ob_log. PendingFill entries use pending_seq namespace. ob_archiver_pending stores pending actions separately from ob_archiver_store.

techniques.name: commutative state aggregation replacing linearizability
techniques.description: when state updates are cumulative and commutative, replace linearizable replication with eventual consistency via gossip
preconditions: state updates form a commutative monoid
application tolerates transient inconsistency between replicas
view completeness matters more than ordering
techniques.generalized_form: for a set of state updates U, if apply order is irrelevant, replace linearizable replication with eventual consistency. final states converge once all updates are delivered.
techniques.applicability_notes: medium: OMS aggregates effects from CE and OB into oms_log. oms_ce_cursor and oms_ob_cursor track per-source progress independently. OMS muting (oms_muted) prevents double-application. question: are OMS state aggregations commutative? if OB fills commute with CE snapshots, OMS replication could be relaxed.

techniques.name: broadcast replication for zero-latency failover
techniques.description: broadcast all state mutations to all replicas regardless of primary/backup role, so that promotion is instantaneous
preconditions: state is small enough to replicate to all nodes
failover latency is more critical than bandwidth cost
role distinction only governs who serves requests, not who has state
techniques.generalized_form: broadcast all mutations to all replicas. on failover, the new primary already has current state. promotion cost drops from O(state_size) catch-up to O(1) role reassignment.

applications.notes: Found real liveness bug: OBViewChange doesn't re-tag/re-broadcast recovered effects with new session. OMS permanently stuck after OB view change. Fix: re-tag and re-broadcast.

techniques.applicability_notes: high: msgs_ce_broadcast is broadcast to all consumers (backup + OB + OMS). ce_backup_applied tracks what backup has received. CEViewChange anchors on ce_backup_applied if backup is alive. this is the broadcast replication pattern. failover speed depends on backup being current.

techniques.name: multi-source vector clocking with per-source cursors
techniques.description: when multiple independent sources update the same entity, track a per-source timestamp cursor and reject updates older than that source's cursor
preconditions: update streams from different sources are logically independent
each source has an independent monotonic timestamp
only same-source staleness is a concern
techniques.generalized_form: for entity E updated by sources {S_1, ..., S_k}, maintain cursor vector [t_1, ..., t_k]. when update from S_i arrives with timestamp t: if t <= t_i, reject (stale). else accept and set t_i = t.

applications.notes: Added ob_cached_ce_session to gate OBReceiveMFR against stale MFRs from rolled-back CE sessions. Fixes semantic gap where phantom CEFinalization could be produced for rolled-back fills.
Added ob_session echo in CEProcessFill MFR message + guard in OBReceiveMFR. Prevents phantom CEFinalization from stale OB session fills after OBViewChange.

INV11 strengthened from vacuous >= 0 checks to log-bounded + session-consistency + seq-uniqueness conjuncts. Mirrors INV9/INV10 patterns. OMS log was least-constrained log in spec; gap closed. No state or action changes.

known_failures.reason: P15 deliberately handles old-session pending fills. CEProcessRecoveredPending has msgs_ce_to_ob in UNCHANGED, so phantom CEFinalization claim is baseless.

techniques.applicability_notes: directly instantiated: oms_cached_ce_ts[p] and oms_cached_ob_ts[p][m] implement per-source cursors. OMS accepts CE effects only if session matches cached CE timestamp, and OB effects only if session matches cached OB timestamp. this is the multi-source vector clock pattern.

techniques.name: fire-and-forget archival with idempotent deduplication
techniques.description: multiple services independently submit the same data to archivers without coordination, relying on archiver-side deduplication
preconditions: archivers can deduplicate by content hash or unique identifier
submissions are idempotent
archivers are non-byzantine
techniques.generalized_form: if k services each hold a copy of datum D and independently submit D to archiver set A, no coordination between submitters. archiver deduplicates. critical path unblocked because no ack is waited for.

applications.notes: Eliminated ce_archiver_ack_count via session-scoped backup watermark GC. Fixed idempotency bug. Session-aware filter preserves old-session effects for double-crash recovery.

known_failures.reason: Proposed INV20 for OB archiver GC is not an invariant: pre-existing records violate it after view change. OB archiver GC also lacks DA safety justification (no L1 anchor for OB).

techniques.applicability_notes: directly instantiated: oms_archiver_store receives effects without ack on critical path. ce_archiver_ack_count tracks acks separately from ce_log progression. question: does CE block on archiver acks? if so, that violates fire-and-forget and is a candidate optimization.

techniques.name: reactive constraint enforcement on state change
techniques.description: apply mutations optimistically and run a reactive constraint checker after every state change that might violate constraints
preconditions: constraint violations are detectable after the fact
corrective actions are cheap and automated
constraints change asynchronously
trust_requirements: constraint checker runs on every relevant state change
techniques.generalized_form: apply mutations immediately, trigger constraint check after every state change that could affect constraint C, and emit corrective mutations if C is violated.

applications.notes: Added ~oms_muted guard to OMSConsumeCE, OMSConsumeOB, OMSConsumePending. Prevents consumption while muted that would be wiped by OMSViewChange.

techniques.applicability_notes: medium: CEProcessFill validates fill before producing MFR+Snapshot pair. OB produces fills optimistically and CE reacts. question: are there constraint checks in CE that could be deferred to reactive post-processing, reducing CE critical path latency?

techniques.name: uniform effect log pattern across heterogeneous services
techniques.description: impose the same structural pattern (deterministic leader + monotonic signed effect log + fire-and-forget replication + archiver DA) across all services
preconditions: each service has a single sequencer (leader) per scope
service outputs can be modeled as an append-only effect log
DA and replication are separable concerns
techniques.generalized_form: replace N distinct replication designs with a single pattern: scoped leader produces signed monotonically-sequenced effects, fire-and-forget to backups and archivers, consumers subscribe to effect stream.

known_failures.reason: Unified action loses independent WF on backup ingest path. TLA+ WF on nondeterministic choice does not distribute to branches. Backup DA path can be starved.

techniques.applicability_notes: directly instantiated: CE, OB, and OMS each follow this exact pattern (primary seq, log, archiver_store, broadcast). the spec is the canonical instantiation. analysis should check whether all three services maintain structural parity or have asymmetric gaps.

techniques.name: semantically-neutral message batching
techniques.description: batch multiple effects into a single network message for bandwidth efficiency, ensuring batch boundaries carry no semantic meaning
preconditions: ordering is determined by per-effect sequence numbers, not batch membership
consumers can unpack and apply effects individually
consumers can request individual effects on gap detection
techniques.generalized_form: if effects e_1..e_k are individually sequenced and self-describing, grouping them into batch B = [e_1..e_k] for transport is purely a bandwidth optimization. B carries no ordering or finalization semantics.
techniques.applicability_notes: high: msgs_ce_broadcast, msgs_ob_broadcast contain individual effect records. effects are self-describing (type, seq, session). batching is transparent since seq determines order. directly applicable as bandwidth optimization.

novel_techniques.name: fill-snapshot-collapse
novel_techniques.description: collapse atomic MFR+Snapshot pair into single MFR effect when downstream consumers are type-agnostic
preconditions: downstream consumers do not branch on effect type
the paired effect types serve no independent semantic role
novel_techniques.generalized_form: when a protocol produces N typed effects atomically per event but no consumer distinguishes the types, collapse to 1 effect. reduces seq-space consumption by N-1 per event.

applications.notes: Collapsed MFR+Snapshot to single MFR per fill. Halved seq consumption per fill.
